title: armeria maritima subsp. planifolia
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. planifolia, also known as the dwarf sea thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Armeria, characterized by its low-growing and compact habit, typically reaching heights of 5-10 cm. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions, including wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Armeria maritima subsp. planifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or infusing them in oil to create a topical salve or tincture.
